title: eryngium tricuspidatum var. variifolium
intro: Eryngium tricuspidatum var. variifolium, also known as Sea Holly or Eryngium, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, as well as having antiseptic and antiviral properties. Eryngium tricuspidatum var. variifolium is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
